mechanism_narrative: A rising federal funds rate trend, as the Fed continues its 2004-2005 tightening cycle, raises the discount rate applied to equity cash flows and increases relative attractiveness of short-term fixed income, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 2.79% in April 2005, up from 1.0% mid-2004
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS trend and forward 20d returns stays below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or returns following FEDFUNDS increases are not significantly more negative than baseline (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window, it signals an emerging labor market deterioration that predicts weaker consumer spending and corporate earnings, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 5.2% in April 2005, near cyclical lows
failure_condition: In periods when the trigger fires, forward 20d returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, or the average triggered return is not significantly below the unconditional mean (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous deceleration in housing starts and payroll employment growth jointly signals a synchronized demand-and-labor slowdown that single-series models miss, causing S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days to turn negative once both series weaken concurrently.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 3-month change < 0 AND PAYEMS 3-month change < 0 simultaneously
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1837K in March 2005, elevated but off recent peaks
failure_condition: Joint-decline episodes produce 60d forward returns that are not significantly more negative than episodes where only one series declines (p > 0.05), or occur in fewer than 5 independent windows in the sample.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, each additional FEDFUNDS increase compounds the discount-rate headwind on equities and forward returns over the next quarter become increasingly negative as the cumulative tightening magnitude grows, but this relationship reverses once the Fed signals a pause.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS has risen in each of the prior 4 FOMC-linked monthly observations
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS rose from 1.0% to 2.79% between mid-2004 and April 2005 amid CPI near 193
failure_condition: 60d forward returns during confirmed tightening streaks are not significantly lower than the full-sample average (p > 0.05), or the effect reverses sign in more than one full tightening episode.

mechanism_narrative: A persistent upward trend in industrial production reflects expanding real economic output and corporate revenue capacity, feeding through to modestly positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as investors extrapolate near-term earnings momentum.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO index near 118.5 as of March 2005 vintage, showing continued expansion
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO trend and 5d forward returns remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, indicating no short-horizon predictive value.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in retail sales exceeding 0.5% signals weakening consumer demand that has not yet been priced into equities, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days as earnings expectations adjust downward.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 344,895 million in April 2005 vintage
failure_condition: Following triggered declines, forward 20d returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, or the average triggered return does not differ significantly from the unconditional mean (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Steady positive month-over-month growth in nonfarm payroll employment reflects sustained labor market strength and consumer income growth, supporting incremental S&P 500 gains over the following 5 trading days as risk appetite is reinforced by favorable employment data releases.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 133,520K (revised) in April 2005, indicating continued job growth
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS monthly change and 5d forward returns falls below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window, or effect is not distinguishable from zero after accounting for revision noise.

mechanism_narrative: When CPI inflation accelerates while the federal funds rate remains below the inflation-adjusted neutral level, real rates stay negative and equities benefit from an accommodative real-rate environment; but once FEDFUNDS rises above trailing CPI-based inflation, the regime flips and forward returns over 60 trading days turn negative as real tightening bites.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS minus trailing 12-month CPIAUCSL inflation crosses from negative to positive
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 2.79% in April 2005 while CPI trailing inflation was near 3%, implying still-negative real rates
regime_hint: transition from negative to positive real rate regime
failure_condition: 60d forward returns after the crossing event are not significantly negative relative to pre-crossing baseline (p > 0.05), or fewer than 3 crossing events occur across the historical sample to test the mechanism.
